skill.name: ui-component-creation
skill.description: Creates UI components using shadcn/ui and design system patterns
skill.summary: Creates UI components using shadcn/ui and design system patterns
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static command, cryptography, reconnaissance, and Windows SAM findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, design-token examples, and ordinary documentation text. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found. The only confirmed concern is a low-risk instruction to read an absolute /DESIGN-SYSTEM.md path before generating UI.

low_findings.title: Absolute Filesystem Reference
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to read /DESIGN-SYSTEM.md before generating UI. This is a low-risk filesystem concern because an absolute path may point outside the skill package, but there is no evidence of secret access, exfiltration, or destructive behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-read instruction is explicit at line 15, but its purpose is to load a design-system document. No evidence found that the instruction targets secrets or sends file contents elsewhere.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences and inline design-token examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are documentation examples, not executable Ruby code or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are a fenced TypeScript component example and Markdown inline code markers. There is no script file, runtime hook, or command invocation in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Benign Documentation Matched High-Risk Keywords
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance detections do not match the skill intent or content. The cited lines describe UI quality gates, design tokens, and responsive layout guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic logic, SAM database access, network scanning, or host reconnaissance. The static hits are keyword collisions inside Markdown documentation.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent UI components that follow shared design rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create shadcn/ui components with design tokens, responsive behavior, and accessibility states.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of client-side React components using shadcn/ui Card and Button patterns.
Requires design-system tokens for colors, borders, spacing, and typography.
Encourages hover, focus, disabled, and accessible states for new components.
Flags raw Tailwind colors and fixed desktop grids as forbidden patterns.
Directs agents to check brand alignment, visual distinctiveness, and accessibility.
limitations: Does not include executable generators, templates, tests, or validation scripts.
Assumes a project already has shadcn/ui, Tailwind, and the referenced design tokens.
Uses a generic component example with data typed as any.
References an absolute design-system path that may need project-specific adjustment.

use_cases.title: Build Product Cards
use_cases.description: Create reusable product or dashboard cards that follow existing design tokens and component conventions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Feature UI
use_cases.description: Turn feature requirements into consistent shadcn/ui layouts with responsive states and accessible controls.

use_cases.title: Review Component Drafts
use_cases.description: Check generated UI against token usage, responsive breakpoints, and accessibility expectations before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a shadcn/ui card component for a [feature]. Use the project design tokens and include hover, focus, and disabled states.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this rough UI description into a responsive shadcn/ui component. Replace raw colors with design tokens and avoid fixed desktop-only grids.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Accessibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component plan for accessibility, responsive behavior, and token usage. Suggest concrete changes before writing the final component.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Component Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a small set of related UI components for [workflow]. Use shadcn/ui patterns, shared design tokens, complete interaction states, and responsive layouts.

output_examples.input: Create a settings card for notification preferences.
output_examples.output: A responsive card layout with tokenized background, border, and text styles, accessible controls, and clear hover and disabled states.

output_examples.input: Review a component that uses bg-white and text-gray-600.
output_examples.output: A design-system review that recommends replacing raw color classes with approved background and text tokens.
A responsive layout recommendation that adds mobile-first behavior and breakpoint-specific columns.

best_practices: Read the project design system before generating component details.
Use approved tokens for colors, borders, spacing, and text styles.
Include responsive behavior and all common interaction states.
anti_patterns: Using raw Tailwind colors when project tokens exist.
Creating fixed three-column grids without responsive breakpoints.
Skipping accessibility labels or state handling for interactive controls.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets React components built with shadcn/ui and Tailwind design tokens.

faq.question: Does this skill generate files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions and patterns for an AI coding assistant to follow.

faq.question: Can I use it without shadcn/ui?
faq.answer: You can adapt the guidance, but the examples assume shadcn/ui components are available.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for aria labels and complete hover, focus, and disabled states.

faq.question: Why does it mention a design-system file?
faq.answer: The skill expects project-specific design rules to be read before component generation.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only a low-risk absolute file reference and no malicious behavior.
